description: Connecting to a remote Kimai MCP server (Streamable HTTP with OAuth 2.1, since v2.12.0)

steps: 1. Admin: set an 'auth_secret' for your user in config/users.json on the server
2. In Claude.ai: Settings -> Connectors -> Add custom connector
3. Enter the URL above (the shared /mcp endpoint, WITHOUT a user slug)
4. Claude.ai registers itself automatically via Dynamic Client Registration
5. When connecting, a login page appears: enter your user slug and auth_secret

notes: - Start the server behind an HTTPS reverse proxy, for example:
- Each user entry in users.json needs: kimai_url, kimai_token and auth_secret
- Generate secrets with: python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(32))"
- Access tokens are valid for 1 hour and refreshed automatically (up to 30 days)
- Tokens are kept in memory: reconnect the connector after a server restart
- The old per-user URLs https://mcp.example.com/mcp/{user-slug} still work but are deprecated
- Migrate to the OAuth endpoint /mcp and start the server with --disable-legacy-slugs
- The SSE server (kimai-mcp-server, /sse endpoint with Authorization/X-Kimai-Token headers)
is deprecated and not functional; do not use it
- Kimai credentials are stored server-side only; nothing is configured in the client
- Each user keeps their individual Kimai permissions and audit trail
- See DEPLOYMENT.md for the full deployment guide
